conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 10 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Next question:

Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex? In other words, is Alex living with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me actually. They live with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is important information for determining eligibility.

Do you contribute towards Alex's upbringing in any way, such as providing financial support or care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out financially. I contribute towards things like clothes and school expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility.

Does the other parent (the one Alex lives with) currently claim Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just to clarify an important point:

Even though Alex doesn't live with you full-time, do you have Alex staying with you for any periods of time, or does Alex live with the other parent all of the time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent all of the time. They don't stay with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me and the UK government's Child Benefit eligibility rules, I need to give you an assessment of your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While you do contribute financially to Alex's upbringing (which is commendable), the key requirement for Child Benefit is that **the child must normally live with you**. Since Alex lives with their other parent all of the time and doesn't stay with you, you don't meet this essential eligibility criterion.

The other parent who Alex lives with would be the eligible person to claim Child Benefit, even though they're not currently claiming it. You might want to encourage them to apply if they haven't already, as Child Benefit can provide important financial support for raising a child.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 10 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: According to UK Child Benefit rules, to be eligible you must be responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) AND the child must normally live with you. Alex is 10 years old, so meets the age requirement. However, Alex lives with their other parent all of the time and does not live with the claimant. Although the claimant contributes financially towards Alex's upbringing (clothes, school expenses), this alone does not satisfy the requirement that the child must live with them. The key eligibility criterion is that the child must normally live with the person claiming. Since Alex does not live with the claimant at all, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. The other parent who Alex lives with would be the person eligible to claim, even though they are not currently doing so.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because, although they contribute financially to Alex's upbringing, Alex does not live with them. Child Benefit requires that the child normally lives with the person claiming. The other parent with whom Alex lives full-time would be the eligible claimant.
